Great beach with lots of food options. Not busy during weekdays in early February. We had grilled whole fish and shrimp dish here. Very fresh

Beautiful beach. I went here on a Sunday so it was very crowded. But overall water was nice and kids had fun. We rented a table and ordered food at a restaurant called Super. I didnt do much research before going. Our taxi driver recommended this restaurant. Their food was good, we used the WiFi and bathrooms were available at no extra charge. There were no showers. We paid total 1700 pesos which included 150 tip. We ordered 3 pc fish tacos, shrimp pizza, pop, orange juice, 2 nonalcohol mojitos and pina colada. Mojito was better. Staff was kind and service was great. We had no issues with the restaurant. Only downside is the location which is right in front of the boat docking area. It was super crowded. We kept bumping into people and getting splashed in the face. Thats how crowded it was. I dont swim but my husband went swimming a bit far out and said it was one of the best snorkelling experiences. My 3 year old had a great time as well and my 4 month old slept through the noise. The left side of the beach is slightly more shallow and crowded and the far right side is more peaceful.

The Malecón in La Paz, Baja California Sur, is a 5kilometer stretch where you can walk, jog, or ride your bike. Its incredibly picturesque, with charming shops where you can buy souvenirs or simply enjoy the music played by musicians near the pier. Families stroll together, creating an atmosphere that feels like a dream neighborhood. Youll find restaurants to suit all budgets, offering some of the best seafood, including tuna, yellowfin, sea bass, or jurel. Its the perfect spot for a sunset dinner. We fell in love with this city it makes you feel safe, welcome, and well taken care of. The service is phenomenal. It was a truly special time that well always cherish in our memories. comentario: La Paz visit at the end of December 2023. Nice quiet town. Stayed at Posada Las Flores, great location at the end of the Malecon, which. is the center of everything. Hotel was good, location excellent. clean, safe and very helpful management. Do not expect a 5 star rating. We took a tour to Espiritu Santo Island. About 7 hours long, beautiful beaches, snorkeling if you wanted too. The water was kind of cold. you may need a wet suit.We also went to Tecolote beach and rented jet skis, nice experience. 100 per hour. We saw Belentra beach from the boat during the island trip. Beautiful, but empty, no facilities at all. This is a protected area so access is limited. Many places to eat, Bismark is good, Veracruz style fish is the best. Central for breakfast, ice cream at Michocan, try guanabana, exotic fruit, delicious. At the same corner I am almost sure, may be off 1 block there is a hot dog and hamburger vendor, you have to try. Incredibly delicious.

comentario: Awsome beach

Balandra Beach was a true disappointment. Accessing it is unnecessarily complicated and not worth the effort. You need to purchase bracelets online about 15 per person, then drive to a designated supermarket to physically pick them up. After that, you still need to arrive early to secure entry during your assigned time slot. We arrived around 1230 pm for the afternoon entry and were able to get in fairly quickly, which initially seemed promising. However, once we reached the beach, the experience fell far short of expectations. The beach does not look nearly as impressive as it does in photos. The water is extremely shallow, and there is absolutely no shade. Most importantly, there are no amenitiesno chairs, umbrellas, or food options. As a visitor, youre expected to bring and carry everything yourself, which makes the whole experience inconvenient and uncomfortable. We left after just a few minutes and genuinely couldnt understand why this place is so heavily promoted as a mustsee in La Paz. Instead, we went to Tecolote Beach, which was a much better experience. It offers restaurants, chairs, and shaded areas for renteverything you need for an enjoyable and relaxing beach day. I wish there were more honest reviews about Balandrait would have saved us both time and money.

I dont usually write reviews but Im hoping this can help someone who might be similarly confused about the new online ticketing system. In March, we were told to purchase the digital bracelets for entry online. We could not select which time slot we wanted, but we were able to use them to enter the beach during the morning session from 8am. On a Monday, we arrived at 7.45am to about 10 cars in front of us. As soon as it turned 8am we all moved in very quickly, showing the digital pdf on my phone to the staff, and it was an easy process. After reading reviews we were expecting a bit of a hectic tourist trap but it was very relaxed and it didnt feel at all crowded. The tide went out quickly and it was probably possible to walk the entire way to the other side of the bay before noon. Super shallow, super child friendly. We rented an umbrella and two chairs from a vendor while lined up at the entrance for 350 pesos there werent any inside, which gave us the freedom to sit anywhere we wanted on the beach, would recommend. The kayaks werent available for rent when we were there, so if youre super keen on that it might be worthwhile booking a tour.

comentario: Great place even in the overcast day.

Playa Balandra was as beautiful as expected. We chose the afternoon time slot and arrived around 1130 AM, finding about 15 cars already in line. While waiting, we rented umbrellas and chairs from a vendor, knowing we wouldnt get one of the free palapas with so many cars and a bus ahead of us. Unfortunately, the chairs were the type that only allow you to sit straight up, not the reclining beach chairs we had hoped for. An umbrella is essential at this beach. People were let in around noon, and we found plenty of parking. The water was stunning, though the color changes as the afternoon progresses due to the way the sun reflects off the ocean. The water was most beautiful during the first couple of hours and started to look more like an ordinary ocean after 2 PM. I can see why the morning slot might be more appealing. I highly recommend the hike to the Mirador. The panoramic views from the top were breathtaking. It was an easy hike, taking only about five minutes from the bottom. If you pack lightly, you can secure a more secluded spot on the other side of the Mirador, which is challenging to reach if you have a lot of gear. This area is on the way to the mushroom rock.

comentario: This is a very unique beach. The sand is white and the water is comfortable. You can walk completely across without getting submerged in the water. We went to Balandra at the end of April 2024 at 1 pm. We didnt have to wait long to enter the beach, although there was a small line to get in. It was a great day and not too hot. Bring an umbrella, although they sell them at the entrance. People would place their umbrella into the water and sit in the water to enjoy the day. Wow, Playa Balandra in La Paz, Baja California Sur is absolutely breathtaking Ive never seen such stunning scenery anywhere else. But I gotta admit, getting there isnt the easiest, mainly because the locals arent always clear on how things work, or maybe theyre just keeping it quiet not sure. Heres what I found out from my visit in March 2024 1. Youll need your own wheels to get to the beach. Taxis can take you there, but getting back might be tricky unless youve arranged something with the driver. 2. Since its a protected area, they only let in 400 people during two time slots early morning from around 78 am until noon, and then from 1 pm to 4 pm. But be warned, theres usually a long line of cars before each slot, so its best to arrive early to secure your spot. 3. And heres the kicker there are no facilities at the beach, so make sure to bring your own drinks and snacks. Plus, remember to take all your trash with you when you leave. 4. While youre waiting, you can rent chairs and umbrellas from vendors, although I didnt bother. 5. There are only a couple of portapotties near the parking lot, so plan accordingly. 6. Oh, and dont count on any cell service while youre there. Despite the hassle, the natural beauty and peaceful atmosphere of Playa Balandra make it totally worth the trip

comentario: Very beautiful beach as everyone said but some logistics. There are two time slots to enter, 812 pm, and 15pm. Only 400 people can enter each time slot. We went for the 1 pm. We got there around 1145 am. Line can start forming around 12 pm, so I think getting there one hour is as early as you need to go. Since we were close to front of the line, we got to start going in the beach around 1215 pm. So even though the official start time in the afternoon was 1 pm, we still got to go in early. I personally think we wouldve got in anyways even if we arrived at 1 pm based on the crowd. Not sure if it ever got full afterwards, but reading reviews, it can get full where people get turned away. When you are lining up, there will be lots of people trying to sell you beach chairumbrellas. Note that the line forms at where all the people selling the umbrellas are. To enter the line, you go through a dirt road off a highway pass near the main road which someone will guide you to, even tho it feels wrong. We thought they were just try to get us to line up for the vendors. But it ended up being the main line. At the beach, theres bathroom but you need to pay. Therere no vendors besides kayaks for rent. Theres no entrance fee. There are around 20 permanent umbrellas you can use if you are one of the first ones to get in, otherwise bring your own.

This is perhaps the most beautiful beach in the world and wellhidden. Please disregard the commercial website links that scam you into buying a pass for this free park. You do not need to prepurchase a bracelet and pick it up from their store to get into a public park. Just follow the local Mexican adventurers and tourists who are probably just as disgusted with the scams.  As of June 2023, there are currently two sessions to limit the amount of people allowed in at one time. The first session is between 8 AM and 12 PM. The second is between 1 PM and 5 PM. Park rangers will manage traffic at the gate, but it was no problem to get in on a weekday. Parking is free and there is an unpaved lot with many spots. Overflow parking is available, but there is a medium walk in. Some locals choose to use a taxi. The beach has soft, white sand and you can walk all the way across because the water is chestdeep at most. Kayaks are available for rental. There are public restrooms. There are no food or drink vendors. Take out when you pack in and keep this beach beautiful

comentario: One of the most beautiful beaches I have ever seen in my entire life Its a true masterpiece of nature, which changes completely from morning, with low tide, to evening, with high tide. The parking is convenient, but I recommend going early because access to the beach is fortunately limited. Bring water with you because there are no bars It is well maintained and monitored by the Mexican government to preserve it as best as possible. The only disappointment was seeing so much plastic deposited along the roads to reach it, such as the road to Baja California Sur La Paz El Tecolote, which is a real openair dump. With the winds, there is a danger that it could also pollute the precious Sea of Cortez, so I hope that local organizations and the Mexican government can clean it up as soon as possible. I hope that this wonderful and unique nature can be preserved forever and attract tourists who are attentive and respectful of the place.

comentario: Its an excellent choice if youre looking for a convenient, familyfriendly beach close to the city. The beach is wellmaintained and has all the essentials you need for a comfortable day by the water. One of the biggest perks is its accessibility. Its a quick and easy drive from downtown La Paz, and theres plenty of parking available. You dont have to worry about a long journey to find a great spot. The water is typically calm and shallow, which makes it perfect for kids to swim and play in. There are also palapas and lounge chairs you can rent, so youre not stuck laying a towel directly on the sand. The onsite restaurant and food stalls are a huge plus, offering a decent variety of food and drinks. The bathrooms are also clean and wellkept. The only reason Im not giving it five stars is that it can get pretty crowded, especially on weekends and holidays. The atmosphere is more busy public beach than tranquil escape. If youre looking for a quiet, secluded spot, this might not be it. However, if youre traveling with family or want a beach with amenities and a lively vibe, its a fantastic option. Its a great place to spend a fun, hasslefree day soaking up the sun and enjoying the Sea of Cortez.
